title: rubus pubescens subsp. subinermis
intro: Rubus pubescens subsp. subinermis is a subspecies of the Rubus pubescens species, commonly known as the dwarf raspberry or thimbleberry. It is a shrub native to North America, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Rubus pubescens subsp. subinermis is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
